title: excoecaria myrioneura
intro: Excoecaria myrioneura, also known as the poison tree or manjak, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands, characterized by its toxic sap that contains a powerful neurotoxin called phorbol ester. The sap of the Excoecaria myrioneura plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain and inflammation, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In traditional herbal preparations, the sap of the Excoecaria myrioneura plant is often mixed with other herbs to create topical creams and ointments for skin conditions, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health ailments.
